title: Sigmoid Fitting in Excel Using Solver Add-In
author: Kenji Ohgane
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.78ihrue
materials: Basically, no need to change the other options.

steps.title: Download the following excel file
steps.instruction: Download the following excel file. Copy the excel sheet into your excel file if needed. The file contains a sheet set up for sigmoidal fitting with an example data set, for which you can test the fitting procedure. ExcelSolver_Sigmoid.xlsx

steps.title: Open the excel file, and enter...
steps.instruction: Open the excel file, and enter your data in the "x" and "y" column. Delete unnecessary data. The data in the "x" column should be in decreasing or increasing order. Otherwise, the plot will not be correctly displayed.

steps.title: Guess initial parameters for the sigmoid...
steps.instruction: Guess initial parameters for the sigmoid (minimum, max, n, and ec50), which can be easily guessed from the plot, and enter the estimated initial values into the cells (B2, B3, B4, B5). If the initial guess is too far from the real values, the fitting algorithm may not result in reasonable fit.

steps.title: Start Excel Solver by selecting "Solver" from "Tools" menu
steps.instruction: Start Excel Solver by selecting "Solver" from "Tools" menu.

steps.title: Set "Target cells" to "$B$7", which...
steps.instruction: Set "Target cells" to "$B$7", which contains SSR (Sum of Squared Residuals).

steps.title: Set "Changing cells" to the cells...
steps.instruction: Set "Changing cells" to the cells that contains initial values ("$B$2:$B$5").

steps.title: Add constraints if needed
steps.instruction: Add constraints if needed. We recommend to add "ec_50 >= 0.000001" etc., to avoid EC50 becoming negative values or zero.

steps.title: Click "Solve" to run the Solver, wait several seconds
steps.instruction: Click "Solve" to run the Solver, wait several seconds. Close the Solver window when the calculation finished. Then you can get fitted parameters and a curve overlaid over the raw data points. Check if the sigmoid curve is reasonably fitted over the data.

steps.title: Set "equall to" section to "Value of 0"
steps.instruction: Set "equall to" section to "Value of 0". This results in a warning saying "unable to find solution", but you can ignore it.
